description: Create the complete libGDX view layer with all screens, UI components, rendering infrastructure, input handling, HUD, message log, combat menu, and asset loading. This is the main implementation feature that creates the entire src/view/ directory tree.
steps: Create src/view/PlagueOfDanjinGame.java - extends com.badlogic.gdx.Game (not ApplicationAdapter). Manages screen transitions. On create(), initializes AssetLoader, sets IntroScreen as first screen. dispose() cleans up assets.

Create src/view/DesktopLauncher.java - LWJGL3 entry point with Lwjgl3ApplicationConfiguration: window 960x720, title 'Plague of Danjin', resizable true, vsync true. Sets up nearest-neighbor filtering.

Create src/view/rendering/PixelRenderer.java - wraps SpriteBatch + OrthographicCamera at 320x240 internal resolution. Uses FitViewport for pixel-perfect scaling. Provides begin()/end() methods. Handles viewport resize. Uses TextureFilter.Nearest everywhere.

Create src/view/rendering/ScreenShake.java - camera shake effect with configurable intensity and duration (~0.2s). Uses lerp for smooth return to center. All calculations delta-time based. Methods: trigger(float intensity), update(float delta), getOffsetX(), getOffsetY().

Create src/view/rendering/AnimationManager.java - animation queue system. Queues GameEvents as visual animations (damage flash, number popup, HP bar decrease). Plays sequentially. Only advances game state after all animations complete. Methods: queueAnimation(GameEvent), update(float delta), isPlaying(), getCurrentAnimation().

Create src/view/assets/AssetLoader.java - centralized asset management. Creates placeholder colored Pixmap textures (green rect player, red rect enemies). Generates BitmapFont at pixel-appropriate size. Uses ShapeRenderer for placeholder graphics. Designed so Phase 4 just swaps in real textures. Methods: load(), getFont(), getPlaceholderTexture(String key), dispose().

Create src/view/screens/IntroScreen.java - implements Screen. Shows game title 'Plague of Danjin'. Character-by-character name entry using keyboard capture. Story intro with typewriter text (delta-time based, NOT Thread.sleep). 'Press Enter to begin' prompt. Creates CombatEngine and calls startGame(name) on confirmation.

Create src/view/screens/GameScreen.java - implements Screen. Main battle screen containing HUD, CombatMenu, EnemyDisplay, MessageLog. Integrates with CombatEngine state machine: renders based on currentState. Implements the state machine loop from spec section 3 (AWAITING_PLAYER_ACTION renders menu, PROCESSING_ACTION shows animation, ENEMY_TURN shows enemy animation, etc). Uses AnimationManager to prevent game from racing ahead of visual display.

Create src/view/screens/SkillChoiceScreen.java - implements Screen. Displays 3 skill options from engine.getPendingSkillChoices(). Shows skill name, element color, mana cost, description. Player clicks or presses 1-3 to select. Calls engine.processSkillChoice(index).

Create src/view/screens/EventRoomScreen.java - implements Screen. Shows narrative text from EventRoomManager.getEventRoomDescription() with typewriter effect. Displays 2-3 choice buttons from getChoices(). Player selects via click or number keys. Calls engine.processEventRoomChoice(index).
Create src/view/screens/VictoryScreen.java - implements Screen. Renders victory text as pixel text. Shows credits scroll. Provides restart option.

Create src/view/ui/HUD.java - renders at 320x240 resolution. Top area (y:200-240): wave counter, enemy name + HP bar + telegraph warning. Middle area (y:60-200): enemy display + message log. Bottom area (y:0-60): player HP/MP bars, status effect icons, action menu. HP bar flashes on damage. Status icons show remaining duration. Wave number prominent.

Create src/view/ui/CombatMenu.java - action selection UI. Shows available actions as selectable items. Highlights current selection. Shows mana cost next to skills. Grays out cooldown skills (shows remaining turns). Grays out insufficient mana. Supports keyboard (up/down/enter) and mouse click. Sub-menu: '1. Basic Attack' then '2. Skills >' opens skill list. Element icon/color next to each skill.

Create src/view/ui/MessageLog.java - implements GameEventListener. Subscribes to CombatEngine event bus. Converts GameEvents to display strings. Typewriter effect (delta-time). Scrollable history of last ~8 messages. Color-coded: damage red, heals green, system white, enemy orange, crits yellow. Messages queue sequentially.

Create src/view/ui/EnemyDisplay.java - enemy sprite area + HP bar + telegraph indicator. Renders placeholder colored rectangle for enemy. Shows HP bar with numerical values. Shows telegraph warning text when enemy is winding up.

Implement input handling: GameScreen implements InputProcessor. Keyboard: number keys 1-9 for menu, arrows for navigation, Enter to confirm, Escape for back. Mouse: click on menu items, click anywhere to advance text. Input only processed during appropriate states (AWAITING_PLAYER_ACTION for combat menu).
acceptance_criteria: All files in src/view/ compile successfully via ./gradlew classes
src/view/DesktopLauncher.java has main() with Lwjgl3Application setup at 960x720
PixelRenderer uses 320x240 internal resolution with FitViewport and Nearest filtering
ScreenShake uses delta-time calculations with lerp
AnimationManager queues events and plays them sequentially
MessageLog implements GameEventListener and converts events to colored messages
CombatMenu shows skills with mana costs and cooldown states
GameScreen integrates all UI components and bridges CombatEngine state machine to render loop
IntroScreen has typewriter text effect using delta time (no Thread.sleep)
No game logic in view layer - view only reads state from model/controller and forwards input
No blocking calls or Thread.sleep anywhere in view code
Zero changes to src/model/ or src/controller/ files
verification: Run './gradlew classes' and confirm exit code 0 with all view layer classes compiled
Run 'git diff --name-only src/model/ src/controller/' and confirm empty output
Grep for 'Thread.sleep' in src/view/ and confirm zero results
findings: All 15 view layer files created and compiling successfully via ./gradlew classes. No modifications to src/model/ or src/controller/. No Thread.sleep calls anywhere in view code. GameScreen implements InputProcessor with full keyboard (number keys 1-9, arrows, Enter, Escape) and mouse support. MessageLog implements GameEventListener and subscribes to the engine event bus. AnimationManager gates state transitions to prevent game from racing ahead of visual display. PixelRenderer uses 320x240 FitViewport with Nearest filtering. All screens use delta-time for animations.
